design_philosophy.core_loop: Select plays, execute football strategy, manage team roster and play progression through NFL season
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Strategic play selection versus opponent adaptation; simulation accuracy versus arcade accessibility
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Coach and quarterback leading a team to NFL championship through tactical decision-making
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Early sports sim emphasizing play-calling strategy and roster management as core mechanical pillars rather than pure arcade action

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty settings likely adjustable through opponent AI tuning and game length parameters

progression.upgrade_system: Roster management, player trades, draft selections across NFL season
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through season games unlocks playoff access; team improvements through roster changes
replayability_hooks: Multiple NFL teams with different rosters and strengths
Playoff tournament structure creates variable endpoints
Franchise mode replay with different team selections

world_building_techniques: Real NFL teams and player rosters create authentic sports context
Seasonal calendar structure mirrors real NFL schedule

faction_indicators: Team affiliation and loyalty through roster choices
environmental_storytelling: Stadium presentations reflect real NFL team identities
Roster composition shows team era authenticity (1992 season accurate)

note: TCRF source provided only navigation/policy information with no substantive game design documentation. Primary assessment based on genre conventions for early 1990s sports sims. Specific design details for Madden NFL 93 Genesis edition not available in provided source material.
